findings.detail: 7 nodes across two subgraphs: left 'Deterministic system' contains two nested sub-panels (Run 1, Run 2) each as a clean 3-node linear pipeline — start(blue) → auto(purple) → done(green). Both runs use identical labels 'Same Input → Fixed Process → Same Output', which is the load-bearing visual claim of the topic: same input always gives the same output. Right panel 'Non-deterministic' is a single ghost node (fill:none, stroke:none) with 'covered in topic 1.3' as an italic subtitle — correctly implements scope_discipline name-and-defer with no teaching. Governing concept in config title field: 'Same input → same output: the test for determinism' — satisfies title-discipline rule. All labels ≤ 4 words, plain English, trace directly to §5.1 corpus phrases. No concept drift. No bare acronyms. Color palette matches project spec exactly (start:#a5d8ff, auto:#d0bfff, done:#b2f2bb).
